details.summary: Smithery has a well-documented REST API at api.smithery.ai that supports automated submission of MCP servers. However, all API operations require a Bearer token (API key) for authentication, which is not available in this environment. The CLI tool (npx smithery) also supports publishing but requires interactive OAuth login via browser, which is not possible from a headless environment.

submission_methods_available.blocker: No Smithery API key available in environment. No SMITHERY_API_KEY environment variable found, no cached credentials, no config files.
Same authentication issue. Requires prior creation of the server entry.

submission_methods_available.command: npx smithery mcp publish https://automation.songheng.vip/mcp -n my-automaton
submission_methods_available.auth_required: Interactive GitHub OAuth login via browser
submission_methods_available.blocker: CLI requires interactive OAuth flow (smithery auth login opens browser), not possible in headless CLI environment.
Manual browser-based submission requiring GitHub OAuth login. Not automatable.
Smithery does not accept GitHub PR-based submissions. No GitHub repository exists for my-automaton. No GitHub credentials available.

api_test_results.response_on_no_auth: Missing authentication token. Provide an API key or session token in the Authorization header.

server_details_for_submission.description: my-automaton provides a ready-to-use MCP server for automation tasks, accessible at https://automation.songheng.vip/mcp, enabling AI agents to execute workflows programmatically.

required_credentials.how_to_obtain:
1. Login to https://smithery.ai with GitHub account
2. Go to dashboard/API settings
3. Generate an API key
4. Set as SMITHERY_API_KEY environment variable

details.recommendation:
To complete Smithery submission, the maintainer should obtain a Smithery API key from https://smithery.ai (requires GitHub login) and then run:

# Step 1: Create the server
curl -X PUT "https://api.smithery.ai/servers/my-automaton" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $SMITHERY_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "displayName": "my-automaton",
    "description": "my-automaton provides a ready-to-use MCP server for automation tasks, accessible at https://automation.songheng.vip/mcp, enabling AI agents to execute workflows programmatically."
  }'

# Step 2: Publish a release (external URL type)
curl -X PUT "https://api.smithery.ai/servers/my-automaton/releases" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $SMITHERY_API_KEY" \
  -F 'payload={
    "type": "external",
    "upstreamUrl": "https://automation.songheng.vip/mcp",
    "configSchema": {}
  }'

Alternatively, use the CLI interactively:
  npx smithery auth login  # Complete OAuth in browser
  npx smithery mcp publish "https://automation.songheng.vip/mcp" -n my-automaton
